title: Interactive read-only C++ project and include inspection
summary: DansCppProjectIndex displays inferred components, target ownership, evidence-backed include dependencies, cycles, and diagnostics, while DansCppProjectWhy explains the direct include under the cursor without modifying source or project state.
details: DansCppProjectIndex discovers the current project root, asynchronously rebuilds the headless index, and reuses one scratch view per root. Enter opens the first relevant component/evidence file, J exposes machine JSON, and r rescans.

DansCppProjectWhy resolves the current source row to a directly spelled include and shows its spelling, provider path and component, first-party/vendored/system classification, search route and provenance, applicable compilation profiles, condition stack, and shadowed alternatives.
DansCppProjectJson shows the most recently decoded index or performs a scan when no index is available.
A project with no compilation database or CMake reply remains inspectable from configured/conventional roots; their absence is an informational diagnostic rather than a hard failure.
All views are nofile scratch buffers. Inspection preserves the source buffer's bytes, rows, modified state, and filesystem.

provenance.note: Command registration, process lifecycle, rendering, navigation, and cursor include lookup.

evidence.claim: Real asynchronous scans and cursor-dependent include explanation open in headless Neovim while the inspected source is byte-for-byte unchanged.

questions: How do I inspect inferred C++ components and include cycles in Neovim?
How do I ask why the include under my cursor resolves to a particular header?
Does project inspection edit or configure the target repository?

last_verified.method: Focused headless asynchronous command and cursor-provenance interaction test.
